Viral Fever.explanation: Paracetamol effectively reduces fever and relieves body pain in viral infections. Stay hydrated and rest well.
lifestyle: Drink at least 8–10 glasses of water or ORS per day
Take complete bed rest for 2–3 days
Eat light, easily digestible food (dal, khichdi, soups)
Monitor temperature every 4 hours
Avoid heavy exercise until fully recovered
Do not self-medicate with antibiotics (viral, not bacterial)
Viral Fever.when_to_seek_care: If fever exceeds 103°F, lasts more than 5 days, or you develop difficulty breathing — seek medical attention immediately.

Normal Fever.explanation: A mild fever is usually the body's immune response. Paracetamol helps control temperature and discomfort.
lifestyle: Drink plenty of fluids — water, coconut water, ORS
Rest adequately and avoid strenuous activities
Eat light nutritious food
Monitor temperature regularly
Wear light clothing and keep room well-ventilated
Avoid cold baths — use lukewarm sponging if needed
Normal Fever.when_to_seek_care: Seek care if fever > 103°F, lasts more than 3 days, or accompanied by rash, stiff neck, or breathing trouble.

Common Cold.dosage: Paracetamol every 6 hours | Cetirizine once daily at bedtime
Common Cold.explanation: Paracetamol manages fever and body ache. Cetirizine (antihistamine) relieves runny nose and sneezing.
lifestyle: Steam inhalation twice daily (add eucalyptus oil if available)
Drink warm fluids — herbal tea, warm water with honey and ginger
Rest well — 8+ hours of sleep
Avoid cold beverages and exposure to cold air
Gargle with warm saline water for throat comfort
Wash hands frequently to prevent spreading
Common Cold.when_to_seek_care: Seek care if symptoms worsen after 7 days, high fever develops, or you experience sinus pain or earache.

Dengue Fever.dosage: 500mg every 6 hours — NEVER use Ibuprofen or Aspirin
Dengue Fever.explanation: Dengue is a viral hemorrhagic fever. Paracetamol controls fever. NSAIDs like Ibuprofen increase bleeding risk and are contraindicated.
lifestyle: Drink oral rehydration solution (ORS) every 2–3 hours
Complete bed rest — no physical exertion
Eat soft foods — soups, khichdi, fruit juice
Monitor platelet count daily (hospital/clinic)
Sleep under mosquito nets and use repellent
Seek immediate care if gums bleed or rash appears
Dengue Fever.when_to_seek_care: EMERGENCY: Go to hospital immediately if you have bleeding gums, petechiae (red spots on skin), severe abdominal pain, or vomiting blood.

Pneumonia.duration: 5–7 days (full antibiotic course must be completed)
Pneumonia.explanation: Azithromycin is a broad-spectrum antibiotic effective for community-acquired pneumonia. Complete the full course even if feeling better.
lifestyle: Complete absolute bed rest for 5–7 days
Drink 8–10 glasses of water daily to loosen mucus
Eat high-protein food — eggs, dal, chicken broth
Do NOT stop antibiotics early even if symptoms improve
Practice deep breathing exercises to aid lung recovery
Avoid smoking and second-hand smoke completely
Pneumonia.when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency if breathing worsens, lips turn blue, chest pain is severe, or oxygen levels drop below 94%.

Asthma.dosage: 2 puffs every 4–6 hours as needed; Budesonide 200mcg inhaler twice daily for control
Asthma.duration: Ongoing management — consult pulmonologist for long-term plan
Asthma.explanation: Salbutamol (reliever inhaler) opens airways during an attack. Budesonide (controller) reduces airway inflammation daily.
lifestyle: Identify and avoid personal triggers (dust, pollen, smoke, pets)
Keep windows closed during high pollen seasons
Use air purifier at home if possible
Practice breathing exercises (diaphragmatic breathing) daily
Never smoke or allow smoking near you
Carry your reliever inhaler at all times
Asthma.when_to_seek_care: Call emergency services if inhaler provides no relief, breathing is very fast and labored, or lips/fingertips turn blue.

Anemia.dosage: Ferrous Sulfate 325mg twice daily with Vitamin C | Folic Acid 5mg once daily
Anemia.explanation: Iron supplements replenish iron stores. Vitamin C enhances absorption. Folic acid supports red blood cell production.
lifestyle: Eat iron-rich foods — spinach, lentils, red meat, beans, tofu
Take iron tablets with citrus juice (Vitamin C boosts absorption)
Avoid tea/coffee 1 hour before and after iron tablets
Cook in iron cookware when possible
Get adequate sleep — 7–8 hours per night
Light exercise like walking improves oxygen circulation
Anemia.when_to_seek_care: Seek care if dizziness is severe, heart palpitations occur, or hemoglobin is below 8g/dL — may need IV iron or transfusion.

Diabetes.dosage: Metformin 500mg twice daily with meals (initial dose)
Diabetes.explanation: Metformin reduces liver glucose production and improves insulin sensitivity. Dose adjusted by doctor based on HbA1c levels.
lifestyle: Follow a low-glycemic diet — reduce white rice, sugar, refined carbs
Walk 30 minutes daily — exercise lowers blood sugar naturally
Monitor blood glucose levels morning and evening
Avoid sugary drinks, sweets, and processed foods
Eat small frequent meals every 3–4 hours
Check feet daily for wounds (diabetes reduces healing)
Diabetes.when_to_seek_care: Seek care if blood sugar exceeds 300 mg/dL, you feel confused/drowsy, or experience chest pain or difficulty breathing.

Hypertension.duration: Lifelong management — never stop without doctor's advice
Hypertension.explanation: Amlodipine relaxes blood vessels and reduces blood pressure. May be combined with Losartan 50mg based on severity.
lifestyle: Reduce salt intake to less than 5g per day (avoid pickles, papad)
Eat a DASH diet — fruits, vegetables, whole grains, low-fat dairy
Exercise moderately — 30 min brisk walking 5 days/week
Quit smoking and limit alcohol completely
Manage stress — meditation, yoga, or deep breathing daily
Monitor blood pressure at home twice daily
Hypertension.when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency if BP exceeds 180/120 mmHg, you have sudden severe headache, chest pain, or vision loss.

Gastritis.explanation: Omeprazole reduces stomach acid production, allowing the gastric lining to heal.
lifestyle: Eat small meals every 3–4 hours — avoid skipping meals
Avoid spicy, oily, and acidic foods
Limit coffee, tea, and carbonated drinks
Do not take painkillers (NSAIDs) on empty stomach
Eat slowly and chew food well
Sleep with head slightly elevated if you experience reflux
Gastritis.when_to_seek_care: Seek care if you vomit blood, pass black stools, or have severe stomach pain that doesn't resolve.

Migraine.dosage: Sumatriptan 50mg at onset of migraine | Paracetamol 1g as needed
Migraine.explanation: Sumatriptan is a triptan that selectively constricts dilated blood vessels causing migraine pain.
lifestyle: Maintain a headache diary to identify triggers (stress, food, sleep)
Sleep at consistent times — avoid over-sleeping on weekends
Stay hydrated — dehydration is a major trigger
Avoid migraine triggers: alcohol, aged cheese, strong perfumes
Manage stress with regular meditation or relaxation techniques
Rest in a dark, quiet room during a migraine attack
Migraine.when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency if migraine is the 'worst headache of your life', accompanied by fever/neck stiffness, or loss of vision/speech.

Bacterial Infection.dosage: Amoxicillin 500mg three times daily | Azithromycin 250mg once daily
Bacterial Infection.explanation: Amoxicillin is a broad-spectrum antibiotic effective for most bacterial infections. Never stop early.
lifestyle: Complete the full antibiotic course even if you feel better
Rest adequately — 8+ hours of sleep
Stay hydrated — drink 8–10 glasses of water
Eat probiotic foods (curd/yogurt) to maintain gut health
Practice good hand hygiene to prevent spread
Avoid sharing utensils or personal items
Bacterial Infection.when_to_seek_care: Seek care if fever persists after 48 hours of antibiotics, or if symptoms worsen significantly.
